channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Blocked drain at Whitworth Road - Herbert Road Junction, 20th June
item.description:
Photos taken 20th June 2026. Drain is on the southbound junction as you turn off Herbert Road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Herbert Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins left out incorrectly now attracting fly tipping, 20th June
item.description:
140 Chatsworth Rd Flats 1-4 have left their bins overflowing outside 127 Elderfield Rd blocking our view from balcony, attracting flies and foxes. It’s now attracting fly tipping and litter is spilling onto our grass. Please can this be picked up,

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Elderfield Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bin locker & infestation, 20th June
item.description:
We have an overflowing bin locker, which needs emptying. This used to be locked but now it isn’t and people have been using it even though it never gets emptied. Now there is an infestation of mice and flies. Please can you clean it and lock it?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queensbridge Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned vehicle, 20th June
item.description:
Untaxed and no mot been here around a month
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Belvedere Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown grass and weeds, 20th June
item.description:
Area has not been cut or maintained in a long time where as close by patches have been maintained.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Pauls Cray Road, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Drunk 14-16 year olds causing damage, 20th June
item.description:
There were a group of ten teenagers in the park around 4pm-6pm who were all underage drinking (aged 14-16) from large vodka bottles. Once drunk they broke at least two sections of the newly installed fence and then trampled the flower beds.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rochester Avenue, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hackney Council employee skiving off work laying down in van doing no work for over two. Hackney Council say they are building better homes no wonder why there's a long waiting list because their employees are skiving off sleeping all day. Where can I sign up I want to be on the payroll and do nothing as well. Hackney Council are only good for budget jobs. No good quality work from Hackney Council tradesmen better off doing it yourself. Hackney Council only do cheap repairs. Do not like to spend money to make better homes., 20th June
item.description:
Hackney Council employees skiving off work laying down in van. Residence pay service charge and this is what it goes to lazy workman who do nothing. Hackney Council doing no maintenance budge up jobs. Seek legal advice.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hillman Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Danger of new fence installation damaging our plants, 20th June
item.description:
The owners of this house are planning to install a new fence at the end of the month. To get to the parks trees that they want to cut down they will have to walk over our flower beds and shrubs. They are also considering throwing the large cut branches/trunks into the park. I cannot see how works can be undertaken without destroying our shrubs and plants. Please monitor the situation. We are also concerned that it is still bird nesting season and will be up until the beginning of September. We have lots of nesting birds all around the park, including this area. Again, we are concerned.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rochester Avenue, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped fridges and kitchen appliances dumpedd, 20th June
item.description:
Two fridges and metal appliaces dumped today outside

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Riverdale Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The lawn grass needs cutting., 20th June
item.description:
All the sun and rain has caused the grass to grow much quicker than expected. Please can our grass be cut. Thanks.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rochester Avenue, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: All litter bins overflowing again, 20th June
item.description:
Please can the bins be emptied as soon as possible as they are all overflowing again. Any chance of bigger bins or more bins as these three can’t cope!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rochester Avenue, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Truck, 20th June
item.description:
Abandoned Truck left on private property for roughly 3 weeks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): William Booth Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Metal oven and clothes lying on the street, 20th June
item.description:
Clothes dumped on the street, oven dumped all on the same part of the road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Clifton Crescent, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 50 cent in the House people Hillman Street. Housing manager Simon Priscilla has got more time for his hobbies of R&B soul campaign then helping residents of hackney. Simon Priscilla is a bad housing officer self-centered person but is good at biging up soul music,R&B least he's good at something needs to give up his day job. Big up salmon Priscilla R&B Star. Simon Priscilla is in the wrong career. Priscilla could employ his coworker Denise Corbin to be his fashion designer,model because she is also in the wrong career. Two piece in a pot. Create a business plan., 20th June
item.description:
50 cent in the House people Hillman Street. Housing manager Simon Priscilla has got more time for his hobbies of R&B soul campaign then helping residents of hackney. Simon Priscilla is a bad housing officer self-centered person but is good at biging up so

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Reading Lane, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Marylebone Lane Drinking Fountain, 20th June
item.description:
Drinking Fountain located in the centre of the Lane opposite #7 & 8. It has been vandalised, filled with rubbish & switched off ! An absolute disgrace that this pretty & useful public amenity has been left to fall into disrepair. Has the council lost its pride ? Please fix it
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Marylebone Lane, Mayfair, City of Westminster
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flannery court, 20th June
item.description:
Gas room door broken

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Drummond Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 17, Parkstone Road, Peckham, Greater London

item.description:
Please arrange

item.title: Broken glass on pavement, 20th June
item.description:
Broken glass on pavement near fence
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chesham Road, Penge, Greater London

item.title: Homeless junkies drug users using drugs in public park in front of public families users. They have set up a BMB in the bush skatepark area. Sofas beds and household items but open air living. Rent free. Someone from Hackney Council could please address this issue which is happening in the public park where families use a lot this is unacceptable., 20th June
item.description:
Drug users using drugs in front of member public on family days out in park. In front of small children.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lee Conservancy Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
